{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks which artery is palpable at the anterior border of the masseter muscle. From the provided documents, several arteries are discussed in relation to the masseter and surrounding structures.

- The facial artery is described as traveling superficially over the mandible and specifically 'following an upward trajectory over the mandibular body as it courses along the masseter's anteroinferior border.' Document [22] explicitly states that the facial artery 'courses along the masseter's anteroinferior border.' This directly matches the anatomical location described in the question.

- The maxillary artery supplies the masseter muscle via the masseteric artery (Document [21]), but it does not course along the anterior border of the masseter. Instead, it runs more laterally and deeper, supplying deep structures.

- The lingual artery is located in the submandibular triangle and supplies the tongue and oral floor (Document [4]), not the masseter.

- The superficial temporal artery runs in the temporal region and is not described as being near the anterior border of the masseter.

Therefore, the facial artery is the only one described as running along the anterior border of the masseter, making it palpable in that location.", "answer_choice": "B"}
